immutable_core.semantic_definition: A one-row-per-ICU-stay baseline snapshot retaining one selected cleaned body-weight event in kilograms inside the approved ICU-admission-anchor baseline window, rather than a raw weight event stream, a patient-level stable weight, a hospital-admission baseline, a discharge weight, or a follow-up weight.
immutable_core.semantic_grain: one row per anchor-qualified summary target
immutable_core.same_name_cross_database_rule: Any database asset published as std_weight_icu_baseline must preserve ICU-start baseline stay-level meaning, kilogram representation, and explicit ICU-anchor/window/selection semantics. It must not silently widen to generic patient-level weight, shift to hospital-admission baseline weight, or use grouped/proxy source values without a separate reviewed identity.

summary_contract.window_start_rule: start at the later of hospital admission timestamp and ICU admission timestamp minus 360 minutes
summary_contract.window_end_rule: end at the earliest of ICU discharge timestamp, hospital discharge timestamp, and ICU admission timestamp plus 360 minutes
summary_contract.qualifying_row_rule: a qualifying source row is a cleaned weight event with a direct ICU stay identifier, a non-null measurement timestamp, and a measurement timestamp inside the approved ICU-baseline window
summary_contract.selection_rule: select the qualifying event with the smallest absolute time distance to the ICU admission timestamp
summary_contract.tie_break_rule: if multiple qualifying events are tied on time distance, prefer admission_weight_kg, then admission_weight_lbs converted to kg, then daily_weight_kg; if still tied, prefer earlier measurement time and then stable source_measurement_id order
summary_contract.no_source_row_action: do not retain a target ICU stay row when no qualifying baseline event exists under the approved source and window rules
summary_contract.partial_window_action: clip the baseline window by hospital admission, ICU outtime, and hospital discharge boundaries

canonical_representation.range_role_note: The hard range matches the approved upstream event-level cleaning envelope. Values outside the plausible band require review but are not automatically impossible in critically ill adult records.

external_alignment.alignment_policy: Use external terminologies as semantic anchors when reliable, but do not make them the backbone of the standard-variable system.
external_alignment.public_anchor_status: broader body-weight observation anchors exist, but the ICU-baseline stay snapshot semantics are governed by this internal anchor/window contract
external_alignment.note: The external body-weight concept is a useful semantic anchor, but it does not by itself encode the ICU-baseline window, stay-level target grain, or no-source-row behavior.

new_variable_trigger_examples: changing std_weight_icu_baseline from ICU-start baseline stay weight to generic subject-level body weight
changing the anchor from ICU admission start to hospital admission start, discharge time, or arbitrary nearest weight
using grouped/proxy source values as if they were exact continuous measured weight
version_bump_trigger_examples: adding a new reviewed database mapping while preserving the same ICU-baseline meaning
tightening the source priority list or validation range after review without changing the baseline window semantics

allowed_database_specific_variation: raw source tables and source codes used to find local weight measurements
unit conversion route before canonical kilogram output
local structural keys used as provenance fields
disallowed_same_name_variation: publishing a hospital-admission baseline as std_weight_icu_baseline
publishing a patient-level stable or historical weight as std_weight_icu_baseline
publishing a grouped/proxy body-size variable as exact std_weight_icu_baseline without a distinct variable identity
publishing a first-day average, discharge weight, or arbitrary nearest weight as std_weight_icu_baseline without creating or approving a distinct variable contract

execution_governance.formal_output_rule: Formal std_weight_icu_baseline outputs should go through execution.py reading this variable_spec and an approved mapping_spec. Execute-mode runtime evidence should record both spec hashes and formal output-file signatures.
execution_governance.future_non_bypassable_rule: Formal std_weight_icu_baseline outputs should be generated only through the governed execution.py path reading this variable_spec and an approved mapping_spec.

not_yet_approved_database_notes: AmsterdamUMCdb-1.0.2 has a related grouped/proxy ICU baseline body-weight asset approved under std_weight_icu_baseline_grouped_proxy, not under this exact continuous ICU baseline weight identity.
